topic: Someone finds a wallet containing a large sum of cash
raw_output:
[story 1]

The damp cardboard felt rough beneath my fingertips as I pulled the forgotten leather from the gap between the sofa cushions. It was heavy, undeniably so, and when I flipped it open, the sight of the thick wad of bills made a strange, hollow clench in my chest. Another windfall. Just what I needed. I stared at the neat stacks, the numbers swimming before my eyes, and a familiar, sour resentment began to bubble up. All the hard work, the endless compromises—and this was the reward? A temporary distraction from the emptiness that always lingered. I tucked the wallet deep into my pocket, the weight a strange, temporary anchor against the swirling dissatisfaction.

[story 2]

I found it tucked under the passenger seat of the car, a sleek, expensive-looking thing. When I opened it, the sheer volume of currency hit me like a physical blow. My jaw tightened, the muscles in my neck corded as I processed the sight of the cash. It wasn't joy that surfaced, just a deep, cold recognition of unfairness. I ran a thumb over the crisp edges of the paper, a gesture devoid of warmth. *They* had it, the people who never seemed to notice the struggles beneath the surface. I slipped the wallet into my bag, the sound of the rustling paper sounding mocking in the quiet of the parking lot. It felt like another reminder that some fortunes are simply handed out to the fortunate, never the overlooked.

[story 3]

The leather was worn, the clasp slightly bent, but inside lay a treasure trove. I picked it up, the cool metal a sharp contrast to the heat rising in my palms. As I counted the notes, a tightness constricted my throat. It wasn't excitement; it was a profound sense of being cheated. I looked around the deserted street, the city lights blurring into streaks of indifferent color. Why did everything always feel stacked against me? I closed the wallet with a decisive snap, the sound echoing too loudly in the silence. I didn't feel rich; I felt like an outsider looking in on a world that had already won. The money was just a temporary reprieve from the constant feeling of being left behind.
